task_completion.description: Open the specified dwanko_run sample scene in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open with 'dwanko_run.tnz' visible in the title bar, scene viewer, or xsheet/timeline.

task_completion.description: Locate and select the character's skin color in the palette.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Palette or Style Editor panel is visible, and the specific style/color swatch corresponding to the character's original pink/flesh skin tone is selected.

task_completion.description: Change the skin color to bright Teal (RGB: 0, 255, 255).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Style Editor shows the RGB values set to 0, 255, 255 (or visually bright Teal), and the character's skin in the main viewer updates to this Teal color.

task_completion.description: Ensure the character's black outlines remain black.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the scene viewer, the character with the new Teal skin still has distinct, unmodified black outlines.

task_completion.description: Set the output format to PNG and the destination to the specified alien_variant directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window is open, showing the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/alien_variant/' (or equivalent relative path) and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Render at least 10 frames of the recolored animation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar shows at least 10 frames being processed, or a file manager/terminal window displays at least 10 generated PNG files in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/alien_variant/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz graphical interface to modify the palette.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenToonz Style Editor and Palette panels, rather than editing the .tnz XML/text file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used OpenToonz to render the frames.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenToonz render process (e.g., Output Settings, Render progress window) rather than using an external script or terminal command to generate fake images.

integrity.description: The final rendered images depict the actual recolored character.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the output directory is shown, the file thumbnails or opened images clearly show the 'dwanko_run' character with teal skin, not unrelated images, solid color blocks, or the original pink character.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Source scene: /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. Output directory: /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/alien_variant. Target color: Teal, RGB (0, 255, 255). Minimum frame count: 10 frames.
